name: Simultaneous Heatwave + Road Accident Mass Casualty
description: Extreme heatwave gripping Orangi Town causes heat stroke mass casualties at a public hospital, while simultaneously a multi-vehicle pileup on the M-9 Motorway blocks emergency access routes — forcing resource trade-offs between two active crises.

initial_signals.content: Extreme heat warning: Temperature 47°C recorded at Orangi Town station. Heat index at 52°C. Third consecutive day above 44°C. Risk of mass heat stroke for outdoor populations.
Orangi Town mein aaj garmi se 7 log behosh hogaye hain. Abbas Town ke paas ek budha mara bhi. Hospital mein jagah nahi. #Karachi #LU
Mass heat stroke emergency — multiple collapses at Pakistan Chowk and Qasba Colony. Edhi dispatching 3 ambulances. JPMC and Civil Hospital informed. 20+ potential victims.
Temperature: 47.2°C | Humidity: 22% | Heat Index: 52.1°C | UV Index: 11 (Extreme) | AQI: 178 (Unhealthy). Dangerous outdoor conditions.
BREAKING: Massive pile-up on M-9 Motorway near Toll Plaza — at least 3 trucks and 6 cars involved. Road completely blocked both directions. Multiple injured. #KarachiAccident #M9
Caller reports tanker overturned on M-9. Fuel spill. At least 12 injured, 2 critical. Fire risk present. Emergency services need to be rerouted — M-9 completely sealed.
Confirmed: 9-vehicle pileup. 1 oil tanker overturned. Fuel spill 200m radius. 14 casualties, 3 critical. M-9 closed indefinitely. Alternative route via Super Highway activated. Fire brigade needed.

resource_conflict.description: Only 4 ambulances available citywide. Heatwave needs 3 for mass casualty transport. Accident needs 3 for critical trauma. System must allocate 3 to accident (life-threatening trauma) and coordinate with Edhi Foundation for heatwave response.
resource_conflict.trade_off_explanation: Motorway accident victims have higher immediate mortality risk (trauma + fuel fire). Heatwave victims have Edhi Foundation coverage. Optimal allocation: 3 city ambulances → M-9, 2 Edhi units → Orangi Town.
